result.stdout:
Description:
  DAX Optimizer CLI

Usage:
  daxoptimizer [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  analyze <path>  Uploads and analyzes a VPAX file in the DAX Optimizer service
  logout          Logs out by clearing cached credentials

commands.payload:
Description:
  DAX Optimizer CLI

Usage:
  daxoptimizer [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  analyze <path>  Uploads and analyzes a VPAX file in the DAX Optimizer service
  logout          Logs out by clearing cached credentials

result.stdout:
Description:
  Uploads and analyzes a VPAX file in the DAX Optimizer service

Usage:
  daxoptimizer analyze <path> [options]

Arguments:
  <path>  Path to the VPAX file

Options:
  -u, --username <username>                     Username for the DAX Optimizer account. If not specified, interactive browser login is used.
  -p, --password <password>                     Authentication token. Required when '--username' is specified.
  -r, --region <region> (REQUIRED)              Region where the model is located. Allowed values: australiaeast, eastus, westeurope
  -w, --workspace-id <workspace-id> (REQUIRED)  Workspace identifier
  -m, --model-id <model-id>                     Model identifier
  -c, --contract-id <contract-id>               (EXPERIMENTAL) Contract identifier. Required when '--model-create' is specified.
  --model-name <model-name>                     (EXPERIMENTAL) Model name. If specified, the model with the given name will be used. If it does not exist and '--model-create' is specified, a new model will be created. If multiple models with the same name exist, the command will fail. Cannot be used with '--model-id'.
  --model-create                                (EXPERIMENTAL) Create the model if it does not exist. Requires '--model-name' and '--contract-id'. [default: False]
  -o, --output <output>                         Path to save the analysis results zip file
  --fail-on-issues                              Fails execution if analysis results contain issues [default: False]
  --wait-timeout <wait-timeout>                 Maximum time in minutes to wait for async operations to complete [default: 10]
  -?, -h, --help                                Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Uploads and analyzes a VPAX file in the DAX Optimizer service

Usage:
  daxoptimizer analyze <path> [options]

Arguments:
  <path>  Path to the VPAX file

Options:
  -u, --username <username>                     Username for the DAX Optimizer account. If not specified, interactive browser login is used.
  -p, --password <password>                     Authentication token. Required when '--username' is specified.
  -r, --region <region> (REQUIRED)              Region where the model is located. Allowed values: australiaeast, eastus, westeurope
  -w, --workspace-id <workspace-id> (REQUIRED)  Workspace identifier
  -m, --model-id <model-id>                     Model identifier
  -c, --contract-id <contract-id>               (EXPERIMENTAL) Contract identifier. Required when '--model-create' is specified.
  --model-name <model-name>                     (EXPERIMENTAL) Model name. If specified, the model with the given name will be used. If it does not exist and '--model-create' is specified, a new model will be created. If multiple models with the same name exist, the command will fail. Cannot be used with '--model-id'.
  --model-create                                (EXPERIMENTAL) Create the model if it does not exist. Requires '--model-name' and '--contract-id'. [default: False]
  -o, --output <output>                         Path to save the analysis results zip file
  --fail-on-issues                              Fails execution if analysis results contain issues [default: False]
  --wait-timeout <wait-timeout>                 Maximum time in minutes to wait for async operations to complete [default: 10]
  -?, -h, --help                                Show help and usage information
